commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5a92d0dac742a17102cfe67134ba05f5f1364d2b)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a bundled Google Antigravity CLI backend plus Google plugin manifest/setup metadata, provider-overlay config recognition, plugin alias routing, model normalization, tests, and a runtime display label for `google-antigravity-cli`.

PR surface: Source +79, Tests +45. Total +124 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect: source inspection shows the manifest exposes `google-antigravity-cli` OAuth, while `models auth login` only selects registered providers with auth methods and no Antigravity provider is registered. I did not run the CLI path because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to choose the Antigravity auth/setup contract and require contributor real behavior proof before this provider/runtime surface can merge.

target.title: feat(google): add Antigravity CLI backend
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a Google Antigravity CLI runtime backend that delegates OAuth and execution to the local `agy` binary while keeping the user-facing model identity on the existing Google provider.

Fixes #84527.

## Scope

Plugin surface:

- `extensions/google/cli-backend.ts` adds `buildGoogleAntigravityCliBackend()`.
- `extensions/google/index.ts` registers the backend in the bundled Google plugin.
- `extensions/google/setup-api.ts` registers the backend in setup hooks.
- `extensions/google/openclaw.plugin.json` advertises the backend, auth choice, aliases, and Google-family metadata.
- `src/status/agent-runtime-label.ts` adds the display label `Antigravity CLI`.

Minimal core adds:

- `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts` allows `google-antigravity-cli` as a provider overlay id.
- `src/plugins/config-state.ts` maps the CLI backend id to the Google plugin.
- `src/config/model-input.ts` treats `google-antigravity-cli` as a Google-family provider for model ref normalization.

## Deliberately Out Of Scope

These are known follow-up issues for separate upstream PRs:

- Status display should resolve runtime labels via the same CLI runtime resolver used by execution.
- `session_status` should handle runtime refs explicitly or provide a clearer runtime override path.
- Compaction should not bind blindly to the current session provider/model; user config can pin a working compaction model meanwhile.
- TUI sticky error state after Gateway restart should reset on connection restoration.
- `current` vs explicit session store key behavior should be documented or made consistent in session status tooling.

## Phase 12 Follow-Up

This PR intentionally does **not** add a SQLite tool-call reader for `agy`.

`agy --print` currently returns text, and any SQLite reader would be heuristic and tied to internal Antigravity storage formats. Tool-call rendering should be handled as a separate Phase 12 design/PR after this runtime path is stable.

## Verification

- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `node scripts/test-extension.mjs google --reporter=verbose`
  - `Test Files 25 passed (25)`
  - `Tests 316 passed (316)`
- Direct `agy` smoke:
  - `agy --model gemini-3.5-flash --print "Reply exactly OK"` -> `OK`
- Source CLI live smoke:

```json
{
  "winnerProvider": "google-antigravity-cli",
  "runner": "cli"
}
```
target.author: kirchlive
